work_unit: deep-audit: strategy sandbox + data sync pipeline + backtest engine
situation: audit-cycle-state was cycle-complete after audit-2026-07-06-024 phantom-fix batch resolution. Triggered fresh deep audit per event-driven cadence. Selected moni-master (quantitative backtest research platform) as next unaudited project — not in prior audit history, has substantive Python/Rust/FastAPI code with RCE surface (strategy upload+exec), DuckDB data pipelines, subprocess-based service management.
action: Deep audit reading 12 files across local_simulator/ and factors/scripts/. Identified 6 findings: H-001 strategy sandbox escape via attribute-chain bypass in validate_strategy_source (AST blacklist) + exec in backtest_engine.py; H-002 factor expression eval with partially-open builtins in fast_t0_backtest.py:169; M-001 DuckDB f-string SQL injection across 11 sites (sync_additional_local_feeds, jqdata_minute_sync, build_market_lake_hot_db, etc.); M-002 PostClosePipeline.ensure_service zombie process leak + fd leak on timeout; L-001 DANGEROUS_PATTERNS substring check over-broad/under-complete; I-001 hardcoded Windows path structural debt. Wrote audit report audit-2026-07-06-025.md. Registered 5 derived_fixes as pending in audit-cycle-state.json. Did NOT dispatch fix this round (audit-only per protocol).
outcome_evidence: audit-2026-07-06-025.md written (6 findings, 2H/2M/1L/1I); audit-cycle-state.json updated with status=fix-in-progress, 5 derived_fixes pending, history entry appended with audit_id=audit-2026-07-06-025
next_suggestion: Dispatch H-001+H-002 combined fix to moni-master worktree (both touch factors/scripts expression eval infra; single opt-worktree covering validate_strategy_source whitelist migration + fast_t0_backtest eval hardening)
After H-001/H-002 merged, dispatch M-001 (quote_identifier wrapper for DuckDB f-string SQL across 11 sites)
Then M-002 (subprocess zombie/fd leak fix in post_close_pipeline.py)
L-001 folds into H-001 fix (same file)

audit_findings.finding: validate_strategy_source AST blacklist bypassable via getattr+string dynamic attribute access or object.__subclasses__ chain; passes through to exec(source) at backtest_engine.py:432
audit_findings.remediation: Switch to whitelist AST visitor or RestrictedPython; remove __builtins__ entirely from exec namespace; add integration test with known bypass payloads

audit_findings.finding: eval(fexpr, {'__builtins__': {'mul': lambda}}, env) exposes pandas/numpy objects in env whose .values.__class__.__bases__[0].__subclasses__() enables sandbox escape; same pattern in 8+ alpha_graph/strategy/*.py files
audit_findings.remediation: Replace eval with pyparsing DSL or pandas.eval; set __builtins__={} (empty dict); pass ndarray .values instead of raw Series

audit_findings.finding: DuckDB f-string SQL injection: con.execute(f'INSERT INTO {table_name} ({cols})') with unquoted identifiers; 10+ sibling sites share pattern
audit_findings.remediation: Add quote_identifier() utility with double-quote escaping; whitelist-validate table_name with regex; centralize in db_utils.py

audit_findings.finding: subprocess.Popen without stdin=DEVNULL, no terminate/kill on health-check timeout, stdout/stderr fd leak on exception path, no pid persistence for later management
audit_findings.remediation: try/finally with process.kill()+wait() on failure; stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL; ExitStack for fd management; write service.pid on success

audit_findings.finding: DANGEROUS_PATTERNS substring match ('eval' in 'evaluate') causes false positives; misses importlib.import_module, bytes.decode, type.__new__, object.__init_subclass__ escape primitives
audit_findings.remediation: Fold into H-001 fix — switch to whitelist AST visitor or RestrictedPython

audit_findings.finding: Hardcoded Windows path F:/stock-backtest/data/stocks_minute.duckdb breaks cross-platform; inconsistent with other scripts using config.yaml or relative paths
audit_findings.remediation: Read from config.yaml or use Path(__file__).parent.parent / 'data' / ...; queue as structural debt (config unification)
